version: WHO Safe Surgery Checklist - Second Edition

items.text: Patient has confirmed identity, site, procedure, and consent
items.details: Verify patient name, date of birth, procedure, and surgical site. Ensure signed consent is present.
Surgical site must be marked by surgeon. Mark 'N/A' for midline procedures or when site is obvious.
Anesthesia machine and medication check completed. Suction, monitors, and airway equipment ready.

items.text: Pulse oximeter on patient and functioning
items.details: SpO2 monitor attached with audible alarm enabled.
Confirm all allergies are documented and communicated to the team.
If difficult airway anticipated, confirm equipment (video laryngoscope, surgical airway kit) and backup help available.

items.text: Risk of >500ml blood loss (7ml/kg in children)?
options: Yes, adequate IV access and fluids planned
items.details: If significant blood loss anticipated, confirm large-bore IV access, blood typed and available.

items.text: All team members have introduced themselves by name and role
items.details: Each person states name and role. Critical for team communication and psychological safety.

items.text: Confirm patient name, procedure, and incision site
items.details: Surgeon, anesthesia, and nursing verbally confirm. Surgery does not proceed until unanimous agreement.

items.text: Has antibiotic prophylaxis been given within the last 60 minutes?
items.details: Antibiotics must be given within 60 min of incision. Document time given.

prompts: What are the critical or unexpected steps?
How long will the case take?
What is the anticipated blood loss?
Has sterility been confirmed (including indicator results)?
Are there equipment issues or any concerns?

items.details: Relevant imaging (X-ray, CT, MRI) displayed and visible to surgical team.

items.text: Nurse verbally confirms: Name of procedure recorded
items.details: Confirm the procedure performed matches documentation.

items.text: Instrument, sponge, and needle counts are correct (or not applicable)
items.details: Counts must be verified and documented before closure. If discrepancy, X-ray before leaving OR.

items.text: Specimen labeling (read aloud, including patient name)
items.details: All specimens labeled with patient name, site, and specimen type. Confirm verbally.

items.text: Are there any equipment problems to be addressed?
items.details: Document any equipment issues for follow-up and quality improvement.

items.text: Surgeon, anesthesia, and nurse review key concerns for recovery and management
prompts: What are the key concerns for recovery?
